title: crotalaria cunninghamii subsp. sturtii
intro: Crotalaria cunninghamii subsp. sturtii is a plant species within the legume family, commonly known as the Sturt's rattlepod or Sturt's crotalaria. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as skin conditions and wounds. The leaves and flowers of Crotalaria cunninghamii subsp. sturtii can be prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal use, and in some cases, the plant is also used in a smoke treatment to relieve respiratory issues.
